items.title: Define memory entry schema and writeMemoryEntry helper in packages/core/src/memory/types.ts
items.description: Pure foundational work: defines the shared type contract and file-lock writer that five downstream memory items depend on. No item in the memory-wiring sprint can safely start until this schema is stable. Unblocking five items from a single eight-dollar task is the best ROI in the backlog.

items.title: Wire /approvals dashboard to GET/POST cycle approval endpoints and add global SSE-triggered ApprovalModal
items.description: Human-in-the-loop approval is the hard safety gate of the autonomous loop; without a working approvals UI the loop either stalls waiting for manual API calls or auto-approves blindly. The server endpoints already exist in v6.7.4, so this is pure dashboard wiring with moderate SSE complexity.

items.title: Fix /runner dashboard — resolve MODEL_TIERS undefined errors, wire agent dispatch form, stream output in real time
items.description: The runner page has confirmed svelte-check compile errors that prevent it from loading at all; a broken runner means operators cannot dispatch agents manually as an escape hatch during autonomous cycles. Fix-priority items with known error messages are cheap and high-confidence.

items.title: Fix /agents dashboard route — render real agents from .agentforge/agents/*.yaml with name, model tier, detail pages
items.description: The agents list is the primary way operators audit which agents are active and healthy; a broken page erodes trust in the dashboard as a whole. The fix is bounded to one Svelte route and one server endpoint with no dependency on in-flight work.

items.title: Fix /org dashboard route — verify org-graph endpoint returns real delegation data, nodes/edges render correctly
items.description: The org graph is broken and shows empty state, hiding delegation topology from reviewers who need it to verify correct agent routing. Like /agents, the scope is a single route pair and is self-contained.

items.title: Wire gate phase handler to write gate-verdict memory entries per cycle
items.description: Gate verdicts are the highest-signal feedback the loop produces; writing them to persistent memory is what turns the cycle-runner from a stateless executor into a learning system. Depends on the schema item at rank 1 and should execute immediately after it lands.

items.title: Wire review phase handler to write review-finding memory entries for MAJOR/CRITICAL findings
items.description: Review findings are the raw material for cross-cycle regression prevention; persisting them closes the feedback loop between the code-reviewer agent and future execute phases. Depends on the same schema foundation and pairs naturally with the gate-verdict wiring above.

items.title: Fix /api/v5/memory backend to read .agentforge/memory/*.jsonl with filtering and descending sort
items.description: This is the read-path complement to the write-path items above; without it the dashboard memory page and flywheel stats card have no data source. Ranks below the write-path items because there is nothing to read until gate-verdict and review-finding wiring ship.

items.title: Wire /flywheel dashboard page to real cycle+sprint+session metrics instead of static gauges
items.description: The flywheel page is the top-level health signal for the autonomous loop; static gauges provide false assurance. This item has no hard dependencies on in-flight work and can proceed in parallel with memory wiring using whatever cycle data already exists on disk.

items.title: Update execute phase to inject relevant memory entries into each item's agent prompt by tag
items.description: Memory injection is the payoff of the entire memory-wiring sprint — it is how past failures actually influence future execution. It is the most complex item in the memory chain and must wait for both gate-verdict and review-finding wiring to be stable so it has something to inject.

items.title: Fix /search dashboard route — implement search backend or wire to existing sessions/cycles/agents/sprints endpoints
items.description: Search returning zero results for every query is a hard usability block for operators trying to trace failures across sessions. The fix scope is moderate — needs both a backend implementation choice and frontend wiring — hence slightly higher cost than single-route fixes.

items.title: Verify /live activity feed subscribes to SSE cycle_event messages and renders with colors, types, timestamps
items.description: The live feed is the real-time window into running cycles; verifying it works is low-cost and high-assurance. If SSE is broken here it likely reveals a systemic issue affecting the approvals modal too, making this a cheap early-warning check.

items.title: Render markdown in cycle phases tab using marked or markdown-it instead of raw JSON
items.description: Raw JSON in the reviewer-response fields makes gate rationales nearly unreadable by humans; this is a small but high-visibility improvement that makes every cycle review session faster. The library choice is well-scoped and the change is confined to one Svelte tab component.

items.title: Update /memory dashboard page to render live JSONL feed with type chips, source links, search, and filter
items.description: The memory dashboard makes the learning loop visible to humans, which is essential for debugging why the loop is or isn't improving. Depends on the memory API fix at rank 8 landing first; once the backend serves real data this frontend work is straightforward.

items.title: Make sprint cards on /sprints page clickable — wire onclick to navigate to /sprints/{version}
items.description: This is a one-line Svelte fix with no backend dependency and near-zero regression risk; it restores navigation that clearly should have been wired from the start. Its low cost and high confidence earn it a slot despite modest standalone impact.

items.title: Complete /branches tab — show autonomous/* branches with cycle, age, PR status, and stale-branch delete button
items.description: Branch hygiene is important once the autonomous loop has run many cycles; stale branches accumulate and obscure real work. The feature requires a git-listing backend endpoint plus a small frontend table — moderate scope with clear value for longer-running loops.

items.title: Add memory stats card to /flywheel — total entries, entries-per-cycle trend, memory hit rate
items.description: The memory hit rate metric — what fraction of audit phases actually used a past memory entry — is the single best proxy for whether the learning loop is functioning. This card should land after the memory API and flywheel real-data items are both stable.

items.title: Ensure /sprints/[version] detail page renders all fields: kanban, success criteria, audit findings, completion %
items.description: The detail page is described as mostly built; this is a verification-and-gap-fill task rather than a build. It is low-risk and reasonably quick but ranks below items that fix outright breakage or unblock other work.

items.title: Create sprint JSON files for every version v4.3–v6.7 missing from .agentforge/sprints/
items.description: Historical sprint records improve the sprints dashboard and give the learning system a richer training set, but the work is purely mechanical data migration with no logic. It ranks near the bottom because it does not unblock anything and carries moderate manual effort.

items.title: Add tests/integration/memory-flow.test.ts verifying cross-cycle memory write and audit-prompt injection
items.description: This integration test is the quality gate for the entire memory-wiring sprint and should be the last item to run, once gate-verdict wiring, review-finding wiring, and memory injection are all stable. Its higher cost reflects the two-cycle test harness it must spin up.
